prompt: Return a JSON object describing a person with fields: name (string), age (integer), is_active (boolean). Use these example values: Priya, 34, true. Output only the JSON object.

Return a JSON object with fields: title (string), author (object with name and email string fields). Use title 'Migration Plan', author name 'Priya', author email 'priya@example.com'. Output only the JSON object.
Return a JSON object with one field `items`, an array of 3 objects each with `id` (integer) and `label` (string). Make up reasonable values. Output only the JSON object.
Return a JSON object with a field `status` that must be exactly one of: pending, active, completed, failed. Use value 'active'. Output only the JSON object.

Return a JSON object describing a task with required field `title` (string) and optional field `due_date` (string, omit if not applicable). Use title 'Regenerate llama-swap config' and no due date. Output only the JSON object.
Return a JSON object with fields: recall_at_5 (a float like 0.85), n_queries (an integer like 30). Output only the JSON object with these exact values.

A user says: 'set a reminder for 6pm to check the benchmark logs'. Extract this into a JSON object with fields `action` (string), `time` (string, 24h HH:MM format), and `note` (string). Output only the JSON object, no explanation.

Return a JSON object with field `roster` (object) containing `slot` (string, e.g. 'coder') and `candidates` (array of objects each with `name` string and `verdict` string). Use one slot 'coder' with two candidates: 'Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B' verdict 'PASS', and 'Qwen2.5-Coder-7B' verdict 'PASS'. Output only the JSON object.
Return a JSON object with field `checks` (array of exactly 4 booleans) representing pass/fail for 4 test cases, all true. Output only the JSON object.

Given this sentence: 'The coder model hit 136 real-gen tok/s with 0.44s latency for 300 tokens.' Extract a JSON object with fields `model_class` (string), `tok_s` (number), `latency_s` (number). Output only the JSON object.
